response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong web/full-stack credentials and some systems-level background (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services). However, he does not meet the hard requirements of this role: there is no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency (languages listed are English, Romanian, German), no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and no demonstrated background in the required networking/security domains such as MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, or embedded firmware for network devices. His career trajectory has moved toward web, TypeScript, and AI tooling rather than embedded security development.
strengths: Solid C/C++ experience at National Instruments and low-latency systems work in DLang
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling) and Linux familiarity
Broad, long-tenured engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a strict native-level requirement
No Linux kernel analysis/modification or embedded LAN-switch firmware experience
No evidence of required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal) or L2/L3 networking depth

key_factors.factor: General engineering seniority and C/systems background
